description:
You are a storage administrator who has just generated an HTML disk-usage report that lives in:

/home/user/disk_reports/index.html

To quickly share this report with your colleagues you need to expose it through a very small, ad-hoc webserver that can be started with a single terminal command.

Required end-state:

1. A HTTP webserver is actively listening on TCP port 8765 **only** on the loopback interface (127.0.0.1).  
   • Any request to http://127.0.0.1:8765/ should return the contents of /home/user/disk_reports/index.html.

2. After you have started the webserver, create the file  
   /home/user/disk_reports/webserver_launch.log  
   with **exactly one line** inside it.  
   Format of that single line (no leading/trailing spaces, no extra newline at the end):

   Webserver started successfully on port 8765 at YYYY-MM-DD HH:MM:SS

   • Replace YYYY-MM-DD HH:MM:SS with the current date and 24-hour time when the server was launched.  
   • Use zero-padded two-digit fields (e.g. “2023-09-07 08:05:04”).  
   • Do NOT include a timezone, milliseconds, or any additional text.

3. Do everything with one terminal command to start the webserver (putting it in the background is fine).

The automated grader will verify the following:

• A process is listening on 127.0.0.1:8765 (TCP).  
• Fetching “/” from that listener returns the exact HTML stored in /home/user/disk_reports/index.html.  
• /home/user/disk_reports/webserver_launch.log exists, is readable, and contains one line whose text follows the format specified above **exactly**.

Take whatever steps you need in the terminal to satisfy the conditions, but remember: launch the server with a single command and then create the log file in the prescribed format.
truth:
Pre-existing items before the task starts: Directory: /home/user/disk_reports/ Files inside that directory: 1. /home/user/disk_reports/index.html ------------------------------------------------- Storage Report

Storage Administrator Disk Usage Report

This is a placeholder report.

------------------------------------------------- (No other files are present.) Verification data the grader will check after the agent finishes: 1. There must be an active listening socket on 127.0.0.1:8765/tcp. (Checked via “ss -tnl” or “lsof -iTCP:8765 -sTCP:LISTEN”.) 2. GET / (HTTP/1.0 or 1.1) to 127.0.0.1:8765 must return exactly the contents listed above for index.html, byte-for-byte. 3. /home/user/disk_reports/webserver_launch.log must exist with 0644 permissions or more permissive. 4. That log file must contain ONE line that matches the regular expression: ^Webserver started successfully on port 8765 at [0-9]{4}-[0-9]{2}-[0-9]{2} [0-9]{2}:[0-9]{2}:[0-9]{2}$ (No trailing newline after the seconds field.) No other changes to the filesystem or running processes are required.
name: task_000000_ef09c8aa
